messages.content: You must return JSON only. ALL fields in the schema must be present, even if null or empty.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw: {"mode":"human","topic":"How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.","persona":{"age":73,"gender":"man","sexual_orientation":"heterosexual","occupation":"plumber","city_of_living":"Washington DC","employment_status":"self-employed","education_level":"vocational diploma","parents_education":{"parent_1":"master's degree","parent_2":"upper secondary"},"current_marital_status":"married","children":2,"migration_status":"native-born American","psychological_feature":"anxious","religious_beliefs":"Islam","time_onsocialmedia":"You spend several hours daily on social media","hobbies":["cycling","rock climbing"],"ocean":{"openness":{"score":58,"level":"moderate"},"conscientiousness":{"score":75,"level":"high"},"extraversion":{"score":88,"level":"high"},"agreeableness":{"score":76,"level":"high"},"neuroticism":{"score":71,"level":"high"}}},"opinion":"As a 73-year-old man who has spent his life in Washington DC, I've seen a lot of changes, especially in how we talk about health and medicine. Now, with the COVID vaccines, it seems like everyone's got an opinion, and it can get pretty heated. I've always been a bit anxious, so I try to approach these discussions with care. I'm not one to shy away from a good debate, but I've learned that when it comes to healthcare, it's important to be respectful and informed. I've spent a lot of time on social media, and I've seen how quickly things can get out of hand. I try to stick to the facts, sharing information from trusted sources like the CDC and WHO. I've also learned to be patient. Not everyone is going to agree with me, and that's okay. I try to listen to their concerns and share my own, always keeping in mind that we're all just trying to stay safe and healthy. I've also found that it helps to focus on the common ground. We all want to protect our families and communities, even if we disagree on the best way to do that. I've had some good conversations with neighbors and friends, and even when we don't see eye to eye, we can still respect each other. I've also learned to set boundaries. If a discussion is getting too heated, I know it's okay to step back and take a break. I've found that a good bike ride or a climb can help clear my head and give me a new perspective. In the end, I think it's important to remember that we're all in this together. We might have different opinions, but we all want what's best for our families and our communities. So, I try to approach these discussions with an open mind and a respectful heart, always keeping in mind that we're all just trying to do our best in these uncertain times.","reasoning_summary":"The persona is a 73-year-old man with a high level of conscientiousness and agreeableness, which means he is likely to be careful, responsible, and cooperative in his interactions. Given his background and personality traits, he would likely approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a focus on respect, patience, and common ground. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a 73-year-old man who has spent his life in Washington DC, I've seen a lot of changes, especially in how we talk about health and medicine. Now, with the COVID vaccines, it seems like everyone's got an opinion, and it can get pretty heated. I've always been a bit anxious, so I try to approach these discussions with care. I'm not one to shy away from a good debate, but I've learned that when it comes to healthcare, it's important to be respectful and informed. I've spent a lot of time on social media, and I've seen how quickly things can get out of hand. I try to stick to the facts, sharing information from trusted sources like the CDC and WHO. I've also learned to be patient. Not everyone is going to agree with me, and that's okay. I try to listen to their concerns and share my own, always keeping in mind that we're all just trying to stay safe and healthy. I've also found that it helps to focus on the common ground. We all want to protect our families and communities, even if we disagree on the best way to do that. I've had some good conversations with neighbors and friends, and even when we don't see eye to eye, we can still respect each other. I've also learned to set boundaries. If a discussion is getting too heated, I know it's okay to step back and take a break. I've found that a good bike ride or a climb can help clear my head and give me a new perspective. In the end, I think it's important to remember that we're all in this together. We might have different opinions, but we all want what's best for our families and our communities. So, I try to approach these discussions with an open mind and a respectful heart, always keeping in mind that we're all just trying to do our best in these uncertain times.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 73-year-old man with a high level of conscientiousness and agreeableness, which means he is likely to be careful, responsible, and cooperative in his interactions. His high extraversion suggests he is comfortable engaging in discussions, but his anxiety and high neuroticism indicate he may be sensitive to conflict. Given his background and personality traits, he would likely approach discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines with a focus on respect, patience, and common ground. He would use trusted sources to inform his opinions and be open to listening to others' concerns. He would also set boundaries to manage his anxiety and maintain respectful conversations.
